--- IMAGES ---

IMAGE 1:
URL: https://pub.mdpi-res.com/materials/materials-13-03711/article_deploy/html/images/materials-13-03711-ag.png?1598058954
DESCRIPTION: A schematic illustration depicts the incorporation of bacterial spores into calcium alginate for self-healing concrete, showing nutrient and bacterial embedding, encapsulation, crack formation, and post-cracking bacterial activity resulting in healed cracks.

IMAGE 2:
URL: https://pub.mdpi-res.com/energies/energies-16-01650/article_deploy/html/images/energies-16-01650-ag.png?1675763908
DESCRIPTION: The diagram illustrates the concepts and processes involved in self-healing concrete, highlighting its composition, mechanisms like crystallization and biomineralization, and methods such as microencapsulation, along with considerations for control factors, analysis methods, and environmental impact.

IMAGE 3:
URL: https://evolutionoftheprogress.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/Polymer-Based-Self-Healing-Concrete.jpeg
DESCRIPTION: The illustration depicts a cross-section of concrete showing microcapsules that release a polymer-based healing agent to seal cracks, along with a step-by-step process detailing how the self-healing mechanism works.

IMAGE 4:
URL: https://www.rics.org/news-insights/building-a-sustainable-future-the-incredible-potential-of-self-healing-concrete/_jcr_content/root/outerlayout/responsivegrid/browseimage.coreimg.png/1706193446670/self-healing-concrete-1.png
DESCRIPTION: The diagram illustrates the process of self-healing in concrete structures, highlighting how cracks can be detected, prevented from causing corrosion, and ultimately repaired through the action of bacteria that produce limestone when exposed to water.

IMAGE 5:
URL: https://www.intechopen.com/media/chapter/72141/media/F13.png
DESCRIPTION: The diagram illustrates how water leakage in concrete causes crack propagation, leading to the dissolution of self-healing granules, which then precipitate crystalline products to repair the crack through a self-healing process.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

enriched_points.expanded_content: Biological agents, such as alkaliphilic bacteria, autonomously precipitate calcium carbonate, effectively sealing cracks and enhancing structural integrity, as highlighted in recent studies.
Self-healing concrete significantly improves durability by autonomously repairing micro-cracks, thus maintaining strength and functionality over time, reducing the risk of structural failure.
By using self-healing mechanisms, maintenance interventions are minimized, leading to lower operational costs and increased lifecycle efficiency of concrete structures.
Self-healing concrete contributes to sustainability by reducing the need for resource-intensive repairs, ultimately leading to a decreased carbon footprint throughout its lifecycle.
